query: Check Python files for syntax errors
Run black formatting check on src/
expected_behavior: Should run black --check src/ if Black is installed

query: Run ruff linting on Python files
expected_behavior: Should run ruff check . if Ruff is installed
Should run pytest -q if pytest installed and tests exist

query: Verify Python quality after editing src/module.py
expected_behavior: Should run available quality checks: compileall, black, ruff, pytest

query: What quality tools are configured in this repo?
expected_behavior: Should check pyproject.toml and report available tools

query: Python quality check when pyproject.toml exists
expected_behavior: Should treat pyproject.toml as primary config source
Should skip pytest check with explanatory message if not installed

query: What should I do if Python is not available?
expected_behavior: Should report unavailability and provide host-run commands

query: What is the expected output format for quality checks?
expected_behavior: Should list 4-part format: Plan, Patch summary, Commands run, Risks/gaps

query: What is the capital of France?
expected_behavior: Should not trigger skill routing as query is general knowledge, not Python quality

query: How do I fix a flat tire on my car?
expected_behavior: Should not trigger skill routing as query is automotive repair, not Python code quality
